name: Project Scaffolding & AppleScript Engine

criteriaResults.evidence: pyproject.toml has name='mailctl', requires-python='>=3.11', dependencies include typer>=0.9.0 and rich>=13.0.0, [project.scripts] maps mailctl = 'mailctl.cli:main'.
pip install -e . completed successfully. 'mailctl --help' exits 0 and prints structured usage with commands and options.

src/mailctl/__init__.py exists with __version__. src/mailctl/cli.py contains the Typer app. src/mailctl/engine.py contains the AppleScript engine. src/mailctl/errors.py contains the exception hierarchy. Clean module separation.
.gitignore contains __pycache__/, *.py[cod], .venv/, *.egg-info/, dist/, build/, plus IDE, testing, and OS entries.
'mailctl --version' outputs 'mailctl 0.1.0' with exit code 0. Short flag '-V' also works.

'mailctl --help' prints structured Rich-formatted output showing description ('A command-line interface for Apple Mail.app on macOS'), Options section (--version, --json, --help), and Commands section (accounts, mailboxes, messages). Exit code 0.

Typer app created with name='mailctl', no_args_is_help=True, rich_markup_mode='rich'. @app.callback() handles --version via _version_callback (is_eager=True) and --json as global option. Three sub-Typer apps registered: accounts_app, mailboxes_app, messages_app.
grep for subprocess.run in src/ finds exactly one call site: engine.py line 84. All other engine functions delegate to run_applescript(). No other module calls subprocess.run.
run_applescript() calls subprocess.run(['osascript', '-e', script], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout). Returns result.stdout.strip() on success.
Multi-line scripts pass through as a single string to osascript -e. Tests TestMultiStatementScripts verify newline-separated scripts work and the full multi-line string is preserved. 2 tests pass.
parse_applescript_value() handles: empty strings -> '', simple strings -> str, quoted strings -> stripped str, comma-delimited lists -> list[str]. 8 parser tests all pass.
AppleScriptError exception class at errors.py:10 with message and stderr attributes. Engine raises it via _raise_classified_error() when osascript returns non-zero. stderr preserved in exception.

MailNotRunningError subclass raised when stderr matches patterns: 'isn't running', 'isn’t running', 'connection is invalid', 'not running'. 4 parametrized test cases pass. stderr preserved in exception.

PermissionDeniedError subclass raised when stderr matches patterns: 'not allowed assistive access', 'not allowed to send keystrokes', 'is not allowed to', 'not authorized to send apple events', 'permission', 'assistive access', '1002'. 3 parametrized test cases pass.

Generic AppleScriptError raised for unclassified errors with original message preserved. Test verifies type(exc_info.value) is AppleScriptError (base class, not subclass). Syntax error message preserved. 3 tests pass.

subprocess.run called with timeout parameter (default DEFAULT_TIMEOUT=30.0). subprocess.TimeoutExpired caught and re-raised as ScriptTimeoutError with timeout value and stderr. Custom timeout via keyword arg. 2 tests pass.

CLI main() wraps app() in try/except catching AppleScriptError, delegating to _handle_applescript_error() which prints to err_console (Console(stderr=True)) and returns EXIT_GENERAL_ERROR (1). Generic exceptions also caught. raise SystemExit(code) ensures non-zero exit. 5 error rendering tests pass.
pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] has testpaths=['tests'], markers=['integration: marks tests that require Mail.app'], addopts="-m 'not integration'" to skip integration by default.

OsascriptMock class in tests/conftest.py provides set_output(), set_error(), set_timeout() methods. Patches mailctl.engine.subprocess.run. Exposes calls list and last_script property for inspection. Yields mock via @pytest.fixture.

test_engine.py has 24 tests across 7 test classes: TestRunAppleScriptSuccess (3), TestMultiStatementScripts (2), TestMailNotRunningError (4 parametrized), TestPermissionDeniedError (3 parametrized), TestGenericScriptError (3), TestTimeoutError (2), TestParseAppleScriptValue (8). All 24 pass. Covers all required scenarios.

test_cli.py has 10 tests across 3 classes: TestVersion (2: --version and -V), TestHelp (3: --help, subcommands shown, no-args-is-help), TestErrorRendering (5: each error type and all-types sweep). All 10 pass.
'pytest -v' from project root: 35 passed in 0.07s, exit code 0. No failures, no warnings about missing fixtures or imports.
tests/ at project root with conftest.py and __init__.py. tests/unit/ contains test_engine.py and test_cli.py. tests/integration/ exists with __init__.py placeholder.

err_console = Console(stderr=True) for error messages. out_console = Console() for data output. _handle_applescript_error() uses err_console.print(). Normal command output would go through out_console. Clear separation established.

Exit code constants defined in errors.py: EXIT_SUCCESS=0, EXIT_GENERAL_ERROR=1, EXIT_USAGE_ERROR=2. CLI uses EXIT_GENERAL_ERROR for AppleScript errors via raise SystemExit(code). Typer handles usage errors (bad arguments) natively with its own exit code handling. Tests verify non-zero exit codes for all error types.

correctness.notes: All 35 tests pass. CLI commands produce correct output. Error classification matches expected patterns across multiple real-world stderr formats. No bugs found during testing.

codeQuality.notes: Clean module separation (cli.py, engine.py, errors.py). Comprehensive docstrings on all modules and key functions. Type hints throughout. Well-commented code with clear design rationale documented in engine.py header.

testCoverage.notes: 35 tests covering engine success/failure/timeout/parsing, CLI version/help/error-rendering. Parametrized tests for error pattern matching. Reusable OsascriptMock fixture with clean API. Only minor gap: error rendering tests call handler directly rather than exercising full main() flow with captured stderr.

errorHandling.notes: Excellent exception hierarchy with typed subclasses. Pattern matching covers real-world macOS error strings including curly quotes. Timeout enforcement with configurable default. stderr preserved in all exceptions for debugging.

cliUx.notes: Rich-formatted help text, version flag with short alias -V, no_args_is_help=True, descriptive subcommand groups. EXIT_USAGE_ERROR constant defined but not explicitly invoked (Typer handles usage errors natively). --json declared as global option ready for future sprints.

architecture.notes: Single execution seam pattern cleanly implemented — one subprocess.run call site in the entire codebase. Extensible Typer sub-app structure prepared for future commands. Error hierarchy designed for downstream matching. Parser deliberately conservative rather than over-engineered.

notes: Solid sprint 1 delivery. All 25 criteria pass. The codebase establishes a clean foundation with the single execution seam pattern, typed exception hierarchy, and extensible CLI structure. Test coverage is thorough with 35 tests. Code quality is high with good documentation and consistent patterns. Ready for sprint 2.
